revision.text:
[[ja:神郷亀塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Jingō Kamezuka Kofun (神郷亀塚古墳) | image = [[File:Jingo Kamezuka kofun.jpg|300px]]Jingō Kamezuka Kofun | location = {{ill|Higashiomi|qid=Q240891|1=Higashiōmi}} | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|10|40.9|N|136|pp|3.5|E|map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 36.5 m
Height: 3.8 m (originally estimated at 5.3 m) | built = Early 3rd century | burial = | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural other_Historic Site | map = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=10|lat_sec=40.9|lon_deg=136|lon_min=pp|lon_sec=3.5|map_dot_label=Jingō Kamezuka
Kofun|mark=前方後方墳-南西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Shiga Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|10|40.9|N|136|pp|3.5|E}}}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qid=Q11397225|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} }} == Jingō Kamezuka Kofun == The '''Jingō Kamezuka Kofun''' (神郷亀塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun located in {{ill|Higashiōmi|en|Higashiōmi|qid=Q240891|lt=Higashiōmi|1=Higashiōmi}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Shiga Prefectural other_Historic Site. Considered one of the oldest {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Japan, it measures 36.5 meters in total length (14.5 m front, 22 m rear) with an estimated height of 5.3 meters (currently 3.8 meters). The mound is situated on the grounds of {{ill|Oka Shrine|lt=Oka Shrine|lt=Okajinja|ja|乎加神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okajinja|qq=}}. == Overview == Initially thought to be a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, the second excavation in December 2000 confirmed it as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, extending its previously estimated total length from 35.5 m to 36.5 m. The surrounding moat measures 12.5 m at its widest near the rear and narrows to 3 m near the front. Including the moat, the entire mound reaches a length of 48 m. A separate moat, located 50 m west of the mound, measures 78 m in length, 1.2 m in width, and 0.5 m in depth. This secondary moat, predating the mound, suggests it may have been constructed during the interred's lifetime as a {{ill|Jūryō|en|Jūryō|qid=Q13859804|lt=Jūryō|1=Jūryō}}. Structurally and ritually distinct from the mounds in {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|qid=Q906708|lt=Kinai|1=Kinai}}, this kofun is thought to have housed a prominent figure of the {{ill|Kunu Union|ja|狗奴国連合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunu Union|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which opposed the emerging {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato State|1=Yamato Kingship}}. It provides a key archaeological reference for understanding the incorporation of regions like eastern Shiga into Yamato's sphere during the 4th century. == Dating == Pottery shards from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} discovered near the burial chamber date the mound to the early 3rd century (circa 220 CE). == Occupant == Two parallel wooden chambers ({{ill|Mokkaku|ja|木槨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) found in the rear section suggest the mound may have been built for an elite couple. == Structure == The mound was constructed entirely from raised earth, typeing a two-tiered structure: a 1-meter terrace topped with a 4-meter mound composed of over 30 layers of soil. == Preservation == As a flatland mound built entirely from raised earth without utilizing natural hills, Jingō Kamezuka Kofun is well-preserved. It provides a valuable reference for reconstructing similar mounds, many of which now survive only as remnants of their moats. == location == Nagashōji-chō, Higashiōmi City, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. * 30-minute walk (1.8 km east) from {{ill|Notogawa Station|en|Notogawa Station|qid=Q1042348|lt=Notogawa Station|1=Notogawa Station}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|qid=Q804135|lt=JR Biwako Line|1=Biwako Line}} * 3-minute ride on the {{ill|Kōsai Bus|ja|近江鉄道バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsai Bus|1=Ohmi Railway Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q129172682}} Kansai or Kakunō lines, then a 10-minute walk east from the Sase Bus Stop. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505809}} * |ja:Category:kk]][[Category:日本の古墳image}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.shiga.jp/edu/content/10_cultural_assets/selection/16_07.html {{ill|other_Historic Site of Ōmi|ja|淡海の文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/other_Historic Site of Ōmi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] - Shiga Prefectural Government Official Page * [http://sendo.fc2web.com/flame02/jingoukamedukakohun/kamedukakohun.htm Report on the Third Excavation Briefing] * {{Wayback|url=http://kyoto.cool.ne.jp/kanta727/singou.htm |title=Report from Jingō Kamezuka Kofun Excavation Briefing |date=20100101000000}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|version=96035938|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:神郷亀塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11590941}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:images of {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Kofun of Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]
